sanskrit: द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च कर्णं तथान्यानपि योधवीरान्। मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान्॥
transliteration: droṇaṃ ca bhīṣmaṃ ca jayadrathaṃ ca karṇaṃ tathānyān api yodha-vīrān / mayā hatāṃs tvaṃ jahi mā vyathiṣṭhā yudhyasva jetāsi raṇe sapatnān
literal_meaning: Droṇa and Bhīṣma and Jayadratha and Karṇa, likewise other warrior-heroes / by Me already slain—do you (just) strike; do not be distressed / fight; you will conquer (your) rivals in battle.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s moral paralysis by revealing that the decisive inner work has already been done; the ‘enemy’ aspects of the psyche are energetically defused. What remains is only the outer enactment, freeing Arjuna to act without guilt or doubt.
psychological_state: Moral paralysis resolved through cognitive re-attribution of responsibility; shift from internal conflict to purposeful action.
trigger_scenario: A team-leader must fire beloved colleagues as part of an already-approved downsizing; being told ‘the decision is corporate, just deliver the news’ mirrors the verse’s transfer of accountability.
anchor_text: The inner battle is won; now simply act.
